title: Track Blood Units by Type, Rh Factor, and Donation Date
description: The system shall record the blood type, Rh factor, and donation date of each received blood unit.
rationale: To ensure proper matching and expiration management of blood units for safe and timely transfusions.
business_value: Improves the efficiency and safety of blood inventory management, reducing waste and ensuring patient care.

source.description: As a inventory manager I need to track blood units by type, Rh factor, and donation date So that I can ensure proper matching and expiration management

acceptance_criteria:
Given a blood unit is received
When the unit is logged into the system
Then its blood type, Rh factor, and donation date are recorded

Given a blood unit with a known donation date
When the expiration date is calculated
Then the system can flag units near or past expiration
preconditions: The system must be operational and accessible.
postconditions: All received blood units are recorded with their respective type, Rh factor, and donation date.
Units nearing or past their expiration date are flagged for attention.
functional_constraints: The system must accurately calculate the expiration date based on the donation date and predefined shelf life for each blood type and Rh factor combination.
security_requirements: Data about blood units, including type, Rh factor, and donation date, should be stored securely to prevent unauthorized access.

non_functional_requirements.performance: The system must handle the recording of up to 1000 new blood units per day without significant performance degradation.
non_functional_requirements.availability: The system must be available at least 99.5% of the time during regular business hours.
non_functional_requirements.scalability: The system should be able to scale horizontally to accommodate an increase in the number of blood units being tracked by up to 10 times within a year.
non_functional_requirements.usability: Error messages must be clear and user-friendly, guiding users on how to correct input errors.

edge_cases: What happens if the donation date is entered in an incorrect format?
How should the system handle blood units with missing Rh factor information?
What if a new type of blood is introduced that was not accounted for in the current system?
dependencies: A database to store blood unit data
A calendar or date calculation module to compute expiration dates

interfaces.name: BloodUnitDB
interfaces.description: Stores all recorded blood units with their type, Rh factor, and donation date.

interfaces.name: DateCalculationAPI
interfaces.description: Used to calculate the expiration date based on the donation date and blood unit type/Rh factor.

test_cases.description: Record a new blood unit with all valid information.
test_cases.expected_result: The system records the blood type, Rh factor, and donation date without errors.

test_cases.description: Attempt to record a blood unit with an invalid donation date format.
test_cases.expected_result: An error message is displayed guiding the user on how to enter the correct donation date format.

test_cases.description: Calculate the expiration date for a blood unit with a known donation date.
test_cases.expected_result: The system correctly calculates and flags units near or past their expiration date.
